data.title: Bitcoin may hit a wall at $84K if bullish conditions don’t pick up: CryptoQuant
data.description:
Bitcoin could hover in the low $80,000s in the near term if it fails to break through its next resistance level, CryptoQuant says, while other analysts predict the cryptocurrency will hit a fresh all-time high within the next two months.

CryptoQuant analysts said in an April 10 markets report that if Bitcoin “continues to rally,” it could hit resistance around the $84,000 price level, but if it breaks through, it could soar before its next resistance level of $96,000.

Bitcoin’s previous support levels are now resistance

“These price levels have acted as price support during this bull cycle but can now act as price resistance if bullish conditions don’t continue to improve,” CryptoQuant said in its report. “This has been the case in past bearish cycles.”

Bitcoin was trading at $79,474 at the time of publication, down 3.5% over the past day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

April has been a volatile month for Bitcoin. US President Donald Trump ramped up global tariffs on April 2, which triggered fear in financial markets, before later abruptly pausing them for 90 days and softening the bearish momentum. 

Bitcoin briefly rose above $85,000 on April 1, but it pulled back to around $76,000 by April 8 due to uncertainty surrounding Trump’s tariff plans.

CryptoQuant said prices recovered “most of the lost ground” on April 9, after Trump announced the 90-day pause on tariffs to all countries except China, which have now been ramped up to 145%.

After Trump’s tariff pause announcement on April 9, both the crypto and broader financial markets saw a wider surge.

Bitcoin surged by approximately 9%, reversing most of the losses it incurred earlier in the week, to retest $83,000. Meanwhile, the S&P 500, which tracks the 500 largest public US companies, closed 9.52% higher, its third-largest single-day gain since World War II. The Nasdaq 100 posted a 12.02% gain over the trading day.

Abra Global CEO Bill Barhydt said in an April 10 X post that it may only be a matter of months before Bitcoin sees its price go almost 29% above its $109,000 all-time high set in January.

“Bitcoin is a levered bet on tech stocks, and all of it is going up and to the right,” Barhydt said, adding that Bitcoin may go as high as $130,000 to $140,000 by late June.

He pointed to the “very significant increase in global money supply” as the reasoning behind his claim that Bitcoin could reach significantly higher levels by “midsummer at the latest.” 

It echoes a similar sentiment to Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts, who told Cointelegraph last month that “the market may be underestimating how quickly Bitcoin could surge — potentially hitting new all-time highs before Q2 is out.”

However, according to CryptoQuant’s Bull Score Index, Bitcoin has been in one of its least bullish phases since November 2022.

CryptoQuant said that of the ten bull signals it tracks in its Bull Score Index, only one is still active, with Bitcoin trading above its 365-day moving average.

It said the market needs to wait and see if the bull signals “switch back on” in the coming weeks following Trump’s recent decision to pause his tariffs.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: China 1Q GDP Print Set to Show Growth on Track:  PollMarket Talk
data.description: The Chinese economy likely had a solid start to the year, signaling that Beijing's annual target of around-5% growth is on track, according to a Wall Street Journal poll of economists. China's 1Q GDP is tipped to have expanded 5.2% on the year despite the tariff turmoil. Economists expect a boost from Beijing's proactive fiscal policy approach, with the issuance of treasury bonds reaching a record high during January-March. Other data due next week could be mixed. Industrial output growth for March is seen at 5.7% compared with January-February's 5.9%, with growth in retail sales and fixed-asset investments expected at 4.4% and 4.1% respectively compared to 4.0% and 4.1%. Economists widely expect Beijing to accelerate stimulus in the coming months as U.S. tariffs bite. The data are due Wednesday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.title: Netwealth Group Seen Sustaining Earnings Margins into Fiscal Year 2025 Despite Market Volatility, Says Jarden Analysts
data.description:
Netwealth Group is likely to sustain its earnings margins into the full fiscal year despite weakness in global markets, with investors seen having "some margin of safety," Jarden analysts said in a Thursday note.

The financial services company extended its net flows guidance to fiscal year 2026 for the first time, reflecting an "increasingly confident" outlook.

"With revenue margin benefits from higher trading activity helping to offset higher cost growth, we believe NWL will be able to sustain 50% EBITDA margins," Jarden wrote.

"[W]e believe the derating of NWL to historical 12m forward P/E levels...provides some margin of safety for investors," analysts said.

Jarden upgraded Netwealth Group to neutral from underweight while lowering its price target to AU$24.30 from AU$24.95.

data.title: Chongqing Changan Automobile Sees Q1 Net Profit Up 12.3-20.9% Y/Y
data.description:
Chongqing Changan Automobile Co Ltd :

  • SEES Q1 NET PROFIT UP 12.3-20.9% Y/Y

data.title: Moody's Upgrades Bank Millennium, Maintains Positive Outlook on Improved Profile
data.description:
Moody's Ratings on Thursday maintained a positive outlook on Bank Millennium's (MIL.WA) long-term deposit rating after upgrading it to Baa2 from Baa3.

The rating agency noted the Polish bank's improved solvency profile thanks to increased capital and liquidity buffers, and the ongoing reduction of its exposure to legally risky Swiss-franc mortgages.

The positive outlook reflects the rating agency's expectation of further improvement in Bank Millennium's credit profile and reduction of legal risks.

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0355 GMT - The Bank of Korea is expected to stand pat on its monetary policy next week. Twenty out of 25 economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal expect the BOK to hold its base rate steady at 2.75% at its policy meeting on Thursday, with the remaining five penciling in another quarter-percentage-point cut following its easing move in February. Despite a rising need to loosen monetary policy settings further to support growth, uncertainty over U.S. tariffs amid forex market volatility will likely keep the BOK in a wait-and-see mode, some economists say. Most economists expect the BOK to lower growth forecasts and cut its policy rate in May. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0350 GMT - The Chinese economy likely had a solid start to the year, signaling that Beijing's annual target of around-5% growth is on track, according to a Wall Street Journal poll of economists. China's 1Q GDP is tipped to have expanded 5.2% on the year despite the tariff turmoil. Economists expect a boost from Beijing's proactive fiscal policy approach, with the issuance of treasury bonds reaching a record high during January-March. Other data due next week could be mixed. Industrial output growth for March is seen at 5.7% compared with January-February's 5.9%, with growth in retail sales and fixed-asset investments expected at 4.4% and 4.1% respectively compared to 4.0% and 4.1%. Economists widely expect Beijing to accelerate stimulus in the coming months as U.S. tariffs bite. The data are due Wednesday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

0328 GMT - China's outbound shipments were likely resilient in March despite U.S. tariffs, thanks partly to favorable base effects, according to a WSJ poll of economists. Chinese exports likely rose 4.4% on year, compared with the 2.3% increase in January-February, the poll shows. Beijing combines economic data for the first two months of the year to iron out distortions caused by shifts in the timing of the Lunar New Year holiday. Economists say the growth was aided by exporters' front-loading of shipments to get ahead of potentially higher tariffs. China's imports likely rose 1.8% last month, compared with the 8.4% drop in January-February. The country's March trade surplus is estimated at $77 billion versus $170.5 billion in January-February. The trade data are due Monday. (singaporeeditors@wsj.com)

0327 GMT - U.S. tariff hikes are set to disrupt corporate supply chains and earnings in Asia-Pacific despite the 90-day pause on some of the larger increases, says Fitch Ratings. Effective tariff rates on China have been raised further and aren't subject to the 90-day pause. This, coupled with the minimum 10% tariff on all APAC exports to the U.S., will hurt companies that sell to the U.S. and Asia's export-oriented economies, Fitch says in a note. The ratings firm sees the auto, tech hardware, chemicals and metals and mining sectors as most exposed to tariff risks. Once the April 2 tariff hikes are implemented in full, exports from Vietnam, Thailand, Indonesia, Taiwan, India, South Korea, Malaysia and Japan could face much higher tariffs, hurting growth and dimming corporate earnings prospects, Fitch adds. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0311 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session on falling short-dated Treasury yields, which typically diminish the appeal of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. There's been a weakening in U.S. assets and better expectations elsewhere such as the possibility of more EU fiscal support, Maybank analysts say in a forex research & strategy report. In the near term, U.S. dollar weakness could persist as these factors may hold up, the analysts say. USD/SGD falls 0.3% to 1.3280. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0252 GMT - The focus is shifting from global economic worries owing to tariffs, to U.S.-China trade friction, SMBC's Hirofumi Suzuki says in an email. "In this context, it would be a blow to the Japanese economy, which has strong economic ties with both the U.S. and China," says the chief FX strategist and head of research group in Treasury. Also, "risk-off sentiment is increasing, and appreciating pressure on the Japanese yen remains strong," Suzuki says. While there's no clear target for USD/JPY, levels such as 140 could become key points of "awareness," Suzuki adds. USD/JPY is 0.5% lower at 143.71. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0236 GMT - Persistent economic imbalances in the U.S. and China need to be addressed to resolve the current global trade kerfuffle, say economists at HSBC Global Research. The U.S. absorbs most of the world's surplus output of goods, while in China, spending by consumers remains too low and investment too high, say Frederic Neumann and Justin Feng in a note. To fix the situation, U.S. goods absorption will have to decline and saving rise, while China will need to curb its investment and boost domestic consumption, they say. While Europe and other economies have a role to play, the biggest adjustment will need come from the two protagonists--and that won't be resolved by tariffs and counter-tariffs, they add. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0233 GMT - Haven assets including gold and the Swiss franc strengthen amid continuing concerns about the Trump administration's tariff policies. "The trouble is, this Trump administration has conflicting objectives and challenging horizon mismatch (between geoeconomic ambitions and foreseeable economic/market outcomes)," Mizuho Securities (Singapore)'s Vishnu Varathan says in an email. "The complexity of myriad moving parts and the idiosyncrasies of this administration warn of a minefield of volatility flares," says the head of macro research for Asia ex-Japan. As such, the "primal instinct to look for cover, underpinning gold, CHF and JPY is understandable," Varathan adds. Spot gold rises 1.4% to $3,218.55/oz; USD/CHF falls 0.5% to 0.8199; USD/JPY is 0.6% lower at 143.54. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0227 GMT - China is doubling down on efforts to firm trade ties with Asian neighbors with top-level foreign visits amid a deepening tariff war with the U.S. Chinese leader Xi Jinping will tour Southeast Asia next week, stopping by Vietnam, Malaysia and Cambodia, the Chinese foreign ministry has said. The high-stake visits come after a top-level meeting this week when the Chinese leadership pledged to deepen economic integration and strengthen supply-chain cooperation with the rest of the Asian region. Senior Chinese officials have also reached out to the European Union, Australia and Saudi Arabia, offering to "join hands" to cope with Trump tariffs. Analysts say Trump's tariff salvos may risk pushing American allies further into China's orbit. The upcoming visits will be Xi's first abroad this year. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

0210 GMT - The Malaysian ringgit is likely to remain resilient next week, supported by the reciprocal tariff delay and weaker dollar sentiment, Kenanga IB economists say in a note. However, the currency outlook could remain cautious as global trade tensions persist, especially with the 145% U.S. tariff on China and a broader 10% global levy, they warn. Upcoming data from China, U.S. retail sales and the ECB decision may drive forex moves, they note. Technically, the pair's outlook appears neutral and could hover around 4.463, they add. Kenanga pegs USD/MYR's short-term resistance at 4.478 and support at 4.421. USD/MYR is 0.6% lower at 4.4400.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0158 GMT - U.S. bond yields have taken another leg higher in early Asia trading. Following a benign U.S. CPI report showing the core measure falling. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that he saw nothing unusual in the markets, thereby disregarding a 13-basis-point rise in the 30-year yield to 4.87% and a 9-basis-point rise in the 10-year yield to 4.42%, providing additional examples of discomfort and dislocation in a highly irritated bond market, says Tony Sycamore, market analyst at IG Markets. Market conditions have deteriorated further in early Asia trading, with the yield on the U.S. 30-year opening 6 basis points higher at 4.93%, edging back toward the precipice of 5%, he adds. The yield on the U.S. 10-year is trading 5 basis points higher at 4.48%, not far from the 4.51% high they hit on Wednesday, Sycamore says. (james.glynn@wsj.com)

0029 GMT - The yen strengthens against other G-10 and Asian currencies in early trade. The U.S. announcement of a 90-day pause on all the April 2 tariffs announcements beyond the 10% baseline rate excluding on China have "done little to alleviate concerns the U.S. may be heading into recession," NAB's Ray Attrill says in commentary. "Amid the return of risk off sentiment, support for the USD is nowhere to be seen," the head of FX Research says. The JPY and CHF appear to be the "only true safe havens" in the foreign-exchange market, Attrill adds. USD/JPY drops 0.9% to 143.19; AUD/JPY slips 1.1% to 88.95. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.description: The Bank of Korea is expected to stand pat on its monetary policy next week. Twenty out of 25 economists surveyed by The Wall Street Journal expect the BOK to hold its base rate steady at 2.75% at its policy meeting on Thursday, with the remaining five penciling in another quarter-percentage-point cut following its easing move in February. Despite a rising need to loosen monetary policy settings further to support growth, uncertainty over U.S. tariffs amid forex market volatility will likely keep the BOK in a wait-and-see mode, some economists say. Most economists expect the BOK to lower growth forecasts and cut its policy rate in May. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Philippines Central Bank Trims Interest Rates By 25 Basis Points as Expected
data.description:
The Philippines' central bank cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points to 5.50% from 5.75% previously, according to a Thursday announcement by Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas.

The decision was in line with a consensus forecast of a quarter-point rate cut.

The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas also trimmed the interest rates on the overnight deposit and lending facilities to 5% and 6%, respectively.

Looking ahead, the central bank will be cautious with any further interest rate cuts. It will rely on data to help keep prices stable and support steady economic growth and jobs.

data.title: In a first, Taiwan charges Chinese ship captain with damaging undersea cables
data.description:
Key points:
  • Taiwan charges Chinese ship captain after February incident
  • Taiwan alarmed at rise in damaged undersea cables
  • China has accused Taiwan of 'manipulating' the issue

Taiwan prosecutors on Friday for the first time charged a Chinese ship captain with intentionally damaging undersea cables off the island in February, after a rise in sea cable malfunctions alarmed Taiwan officials amid tensions with China.

Prosecutors say the man was captain of the Chinese-crewed Hong Tai 58, registered in Togo, which Taiwanese authorities detained after suspecting the ship had dropped anchor near an undersea cable off southwestern Taiwan, damaging it.

The prosecutors' office in the southern Taiwanese city of Tainan said they had charged the ship's Chinese captain, whom they identified only by his family name, Wang, with being responsible for damaging the cable.

Wang has said he is innocent, but refused to provide details of the ship's owner and "had a bad attitude", the prosecutors said in a statement.

Seven other Chinese nationals detained at the same time will not be charged and will be transported to China, prosecutors said, adding that the case was the island's first prosecution over damaging sea cables.

Reuters was not able to determine the ship's ownership or immediately locate a lawyer representing the captain.

China's Taiwan Affairs Office did not immediately respond to a request for comment. China has previously accused Taiwan of "manipulating" possible Chinese involvement in the case, saying it was casting aspersions before the facts were clear.

Taiwan has reported five cases of sea cable malfunctions this year, compared with three each in 2024 and 2023, according to its digital ministry.

Taiwan's coast guard has in recent months stepped up efforts to protect its sea cables, including monitoring a "blacklist" of close to 100 China-linked ships registered to a country other than that of its owner near Taiwan, officials familiar with the matter told Reuters.

Taiwan said in January it suspected a China-linked ship of damaging an undersea cable off its northern coast; the ship owner denied the accusations.

Taiwan, which China claims as its own territory, has repeatedly complained about "grey zone" Chinese activities around the island, designed to pressure it without direct confrontation, such as balloon overflights and sand dredging.

Taipei was alarmed after another Chinese-linked ship was suspected of damaging a different cable this year, prompting the navy and other agencies to step up efforts to protect the undersea communication links, which are vital to the island's connections to the rest of the world.

Taiwan, whose government rejects Beijing's sovereignty claims, has pointed to similarities between what it has experienced and damage to undersea cables in the Baltic Sea following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

data.title: Fartcoin rallies 104% in a week — Will Solana (SOL) price catch up?
data.description:
Solana-based memecoin Fartcoin (FARTCOIN) has outperformed the broader crypto market so far in April, rising over 104% versus SOL being down 2% for the week. As of April 10, it was trading for as high as $0.87.

The cryptocurrency’s outperformance appears despite US President Donald Trump’s seesaw tariff announcements that have wiped nearly $160 billion from the crypto market capitalization in April.

FARTCOIN has outperformed even other memecoins inside the Solana ecosystem, the primary being Official Trump (TRUMP), which has dropped by approximately 25% in April.

As it seems, the third-largest Solana memecoin could rise another 30% in April due to a classic bullish continuation setup.

FARTCOIN bull flag hints a new highs

FARTCOIN’s bullish technical outlook arises from its prevailing bull flag setup.

On April 10, FARTCOIN was breaking out of the channel range to the upside.

This trend projects a potential move toward $0.95—just under the psychologically significant $1 mark—by April.

The relative strength index (RSI) is hovering in bullish territory above 66, suggesting there’s still room for further gains before entering overbought conditions above the 70 mark.

Additionally, FARTCOIN’s price is gaining support from its 50-4H (red) and 200-4H (blue) exponential moving averages (EMA). As long as Fartcoin stays above them, the bull flag breakout may play out in full, potentially resulting in a rally to $0.95.

FARTCOIN mimicks Pepe’s path to a $3 billion market cap

Fartcoin is showing the same signs that preceded Pepe’s (PEPE) explosive run from around $300 million to over $3 billion in market cap in the 2023-2024 period, according to market analyst @theunipcs.

“I'm talking $300m to $500m in daily [spot] volume,” the analyst wrote about Fartcoin while mentioning its absence at Binance, Coinbase, Bybit, Upbit, and OKX exchanges.

In the past 24 hours, FARTCOIN’s volume has been over $446.84 million versus Bonk’s (BONK) $129.85 million and Shiba Inu’s (SHIB) $319.43 million, according to data resource CoinMarketCap.

Meanwhile, Fartcoin is going head-to-head with TRUMP, which posted approximately $661.78 million in trading volume over the past 24 hours.

However, onchain data reveals that Fartcoin is processing nearly double the value in actual transfers, suggesting deeper engagement and utility despite TRUMP's headline volume figures.

As a result, FARTCOIN appears to be in the middle of a powerful hype-driven rally, which improves its interim bullish outlook.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: Market Chatter: Meiko to Build Vietnam Plant for iPhone Parts
data.description:
Meiko Electronics will spend 50 billion yen ($340 million) on a new plant in Vietnam to produce compact substrates for iPhones, targeting fiscal 2026 output, Nikkei reported Thursday.

The move supports Apple's supply shift to India amid rising US tariffs on China. Meiko will supply parts to Indian assembly lines from the new Hoa Binh site, its fourth in Vietnam, according to the report.

Apple aims to raise India's share of iPhone production to 25% by 2027. Other suppliers, including TDK, are also expanding in India to reduce reliance on China, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Whitebark Energy to Start Soil Geochemistry Survey at Giant Rickerscote Prospect in South Australia
data.description:
Whitebark Energy will kickstart a soil geochemistry survey program at the Giant Rickerscote prospect within the Alinya project in South Australia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

Set for July, the program will target hydrogen and hydrocarbon emission hotspots identified across the project area, the filing stated.

data.title: Xi to Begin SE Asia Tour Amid U.S. Tariff Pressures
data.description:
Chinese President Xi Jinping will visit Vietnam, Malaysia, and Cambodia next week, from April 14–18, in his first overseas trip of the year, aiming to strengthen ties with key Southeast Asian neighbors amid escalating trade tensions with the U.S., state media reported Friday.

Xi’s initiative comes as Beijing faces steep U.S. tariffs, 145% under President Trump, and seeks to reinforce regional alliances, especially as other affected countries like Cambodia (49%), Vietnam (46%), and Malaysia (24%) pursue relief through bilateral talks with Washington.

China is rallying global support against U.S. tariffs, with Commerce Minister Wang Wentao and Premier Li Qiang engaging counterparts in the EU, Malaysia, Saudi Arabia, and South Africa.

data.title: Peru Trade Surplus Widens in February
data.description:
The trade surplus in Peru widened to USD 2,109 million in February 2025 from USD 1,645 million in the same period last year.

Exports advanced 15.2% year-on-year to USD 6,318 million, driven by higher sales of traditional products (+13.6%) and non-traditional products (+20.3%).

Meanwhile, imports rose at a slower 9.6% to USD 4,210 million, reflecting increased purchases of consumer goods (+20.8%), intermediate goods (+.2%), and capital goods (+9%).

data.title: CAC Nantong Chemical Opens 328% Higher in Shenzhen Debut
data.description:
CAC Nantong Chemical opened 328% higher on Friday, its first day of trading on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange's ChiNext board.

Shares of the Chinese pesticide manufacturer more than quadrupled from its initial public offering price of 10.27 yuan.

data.title: JD.com Likely to Report Solid Earnings in 1QMarket Talk
data.description: JD.com is likely to report solid earnings in 1Q, mainly driven by the continued trade-in subsidy program and the strength in JD supermarket, say Nomura analysts in a research note. Revenue growth may reach 12% on year and adjusted net profit growth is likely to reach 25% in 1Q, Nomura says. This was helped by JD's electronic goods sales, which likely rose 11% on year during the quarter, the analysts note. The analysts maintain a buy rating and keep target price of its ADRs at US$55.00. The ADRs last closed at US$35.58. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 1.3% Higher at 74835.49 on Broad-Based Gains
Argenx Gains US FDA Approval for Self-injectable Vyvgart Hytrulo Syringe
data.description:
Biotechnology company Argenx said Thursday it secured the US Food and Drug Administration's approval for its self-injectable prefilled syringe of Vyvgart Hytrulo.

The drug is a treatment for adults with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy and generalized myasthenia gravis, who are anti-acetylcholine receptor antibody positive.

The regulator's approval was based on studies demonstrating the self-injected form of Vyvgart Hytrulo is bioequivalent to the drug in a vial. Additionally, human factors studies showed that patients and caregivers could safely prepare and administer Vyvgart Hytrulo with a prefilled syringe.

data.title: ICYMI - Harley-Davidson board member resigns, cites 'grave concerns' about company
data.description:
Info via Reuters.

A Harley-Davidson board member has resigned after raising serious concerns about the company's direction, triggering an 11% drop in the stock on Thursday.

Jared Dourdeville, who represents Harley's second-largest shareholder, H Partners, cited “cultural depletion” stemming from leadership departures and remote work policies in a letter to the board. He also called for the resignation of CEO Jochen Zeitz and two other directors.

Harley said Dourdeville quit ahead of a scheduled board meeting to address his concerns and after voting against the board’s decision not to appoint his preferred CEO candidate.

Earlier this week, Harley announced that Zeitz plans to retire in 2025, staying on until a successor is found. The company continues to face challenges attracting younger riders, and is now leaning on higher-margin Touring bikes and custom vehicle offerings to appeal to wealthier customers.

Bit of an old pic, but they don't change too much

data.title: Agri Picks Report April 11, 2025: Geojit Financial Services
data.description:
Geojit Financial Services's report on Daily Agri Picks

Private weather forecaster Skymet has predicted a 'normal' southwest monsoon for India at 103% of the longperiod average, with an error margin of +/-5%. The season is likely to begin with relatively less rains, as the forecaster predicts rainfall in June at 96% of the long-period average. However, it is expected to increase from July, with rainfall during the month seen at 102%, that in August at 108%, and in September at 104%. According to Skymet Managing Director Jatin Singh, this season, the La Nina weather phenomenon has been weak and brief. "Vital signs of La Nina have started fading now. However, the occurrence of El Nino, which normally corrupts the monsoon, has been ruled out," he said in a statement. El Nino–Southern Oscillation-neutral is likely to be the most dominant category during the Indian summer monsoon, Singh said. The remnants of La Nina and ENSO-neutral together are likely to shield the monsoon from any egregious outcome. El Nino-Southern Oscillation refers to the fluctuation between El Nino and La Nina conditions in the Pacific Ocean. El Nino is linked to drier weather in India, while La Nina results in more favourable rainfall. The preliminary forecast of a positive Indian Ocean Dipole will work towards better monsoon prospects. The Indian Ocean Dipole is the difference in sea surface temperatures between the eastern and western tropical Indian Ocean. A negative Indian Ocean Dipole leads to less rainfall in India and vice versa. The prediction of another normal monsoon is likely to boost hope of robust kharif sowing in the coming season. India receives about 75% of its annual rainfall during the Jun-Sept period, making it crucial for sowing activities as well as reservoir replenishment.

data.title: Senegal Consumer Prices Fall 0.2% in March
data.description:
Consumer prices in Senegal fell 0.2% year-on-year in March 2025, following a 0.6% increase in the previous month.

This marks the first decline in four periods, mainly driven by lower prices for food and non-alcoholic beverages (-0.9%) and information and communication goods and services (-8.1%).

These declines were partially offset by price increases in other categories, particularly restaurants and accommodation services (+1.6%), furniture, household equipment, and routine maintenance (+1.7%), housing, water, electricity, gas, and other fuels (+0.9%), and transport (+1.0%).

Excluding energy and fresh products, the core index rose by 2.1% year-on-year.

On a monthly basis, the CPI fell 0.5%, after a 0.6% decline in February.

data.title: Trump's Tariff Pause Likely to Bring Relief to Asian EquitiesMarket Talk
data.description: President Trump's 90-day tariff pause should bring relief for Asian equities, excluding China and Hong Kong, Nomura analyst Chetan Seth says in a research note. The pause suggests that "we might have already crossed the point of peak-pessimism" and that "there is indeed some pain threshold for the Trump administration," the analyst notes. However, Hong Kong and China equities are still not off the hook yet and will likely lag within the region, as U.S.-China trade tensions continue to rise, he says. Nomura expects volatility in the next 90 days, as there will be back-and-forth trade negotiations. If a U.S. recession is eventually avoided, the risk-reward appears attractive for Asian equities, excluding Japan, with only limited downside in general, he adds. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Dutch Group SBM Offshore Secures $1.1 Billion Revolver
data.description:
SBM Offshore secured a $1.1 billion revolving credit facility from a group of 13 international banks, according to a Thursday release.

The new five-year unsecured facility offers two one-year extensions and an uncommitted option to upsize by $500 million. It was signed to refinance the Dutch deepwater ocean infrastructure company's current $1 billion facility maturing in February 2026.

SBM Offshore will use the funds for general corporate purposes and working capital needs, with a $100 million tranche allocated to eligible green projects.

data.title: Bain seeks to raise at least $7 billion for new Asia PE fund - Bloomberg News
data.description:
-- Source link: https://tinyurl.com/mr5utuhk

-- Note: Reuters has not verified this story and does not vouch for its accuracy

data.title: Novartis Plans $23 Billion Boost for US Manufacturing, Research
data.description:
Novartis said Thursday it plans to invest $23 billion to expand its manufacturing, research and development footprint in the US over the next five years.

The Swiss pharmaceutical company's proposal involves a new biomedical research innovation hub in San Diego, two new radioligand therapy manufacturing facilities in Florida and Texas, and the expansion of three RLT manufacturing facilities in Indiana, New Jersey and California.

The plan includes four new manufacturing facilities, the locations of which will be determined later. The sites will produce biologics drug substances, chemical drug substances, drug products, device assembly and packaging.

data.title: Carawine Resources Signs Loan Agreement With Major Shareholder
data.description:
Carawine Resources entered into an unsecured loan facility agreement with its major shareholder, QGold, for AU$1.7 million, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The loan has a term of 18 months after the first drawdown, with a fixed interest rate of 10% per annum, payable at the end of the term, the filing said.

The proceeds will fund the completion of a heritage survey at the Paterson project in Western Australia and maintain exploration project tenure, staffing, and support activities until September, per the filing.

data.title: ABB Launches $40 Million US Manufacturing Plant for Cable Accessories, Circuit Solutions
data.description:
ABB's ABB Installation Products opened an over $40 million manufacturing facility for cable accessories and circuit solutions in Albuquerque, in the US state of New Mexico.

The Swiss-Swedish electrification and automation group said Thursday the 90,000-square-foot greenfield facility affirms its commitment to supporting critical utility projects and infrastructure improvements in the US.

Over the last five years, ABB Installation Products has spent over $125 million on expansions and improvements across the US. The company also earmarked $15 million for equipment upgrades and automation.

data.title: Geely Automobile at one-week high on robust earnings outlook
data.description:
** Shares of Geely Automobile Holdings rise 4.8% to HK$15.64, their highest levels since April 3

** Stock on track for a fourth consecutive session of gains, if current trend holds

** Stock set for the biggest one-day pct gain since February 27; third biggest pct gainer on Hang Seng Index , which slips 0.7%

** Geely expects its profit attributable for the three-month period ended March to be in the range of 5.2 bln to 5.8 bln yuan ($710.13-$792.07 mln), up 220%-270% y/y, due to record-high sales volume and robust growth in its new energy business

** UOB Kay Hian maintains "market weight" on autombile with top BUYs including BYD, Geely and Desay SV

** U.S. tariffs on Chinese autos (up to 150%) have limited direct impact but raise global trade tensions, posing risks to demand and earnings - UOB

** Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index eases 0.9%, Hang Seng Tech Index slips 0.3%

** Stock up 4.2%, YTD

($1 = 7.3226 Chinese yuan)

data.title: From Joke To Juggernaut: Dogecoin Value Revolution Gets Nod From Global Asset Giant
data.description:
Swiss asset manager 21Shares has openly endorsed Dogecoin, stating that the cryptocurrency has developed a long way from its origins as an online meme. The company cited Dogecoin’s whopping 130,000% price appreciation over the last decade as evidence of its longevity within the turbulent crypto space.

Meme Currency Sees Serious Growth

What began as an online joke in 2013 has become what 21Shares refers to as a “movement” in the crypto space. Dogecoin’s performance, the asset manager says, speaks for itself. The coin has recorded an annual growth rate of 125% since its inception, making it the best performer among the market’s top 25 largest cryptocurrencies by market cap.

The growth is not just in value. User adoption has nearly doubled in recent years, with wallet addresses rising from 44 million to 84 million in four years. Such rapid growth shows that more people are holding and using the cryptocurrency despite its lighthearted origin.

21Shares
@21Shares

Dogecoin isn’t just a meme—it’s a movement.

With 130,000%+ returns, a $30B market cap, and 84M+ wallets, DOGE is rewriting what value means in the digital age.

Explore how culture, community, and memes drive this phenomenon. Read the full blog → https://t.co/wNFYdM2pjS pic.twitter.com/ojfYEkVCwQ

Apr 10, 2025

ETF Filing Marks Major Step For Dogecoin

The Swiss company recently submitted an S-1 form to the US Securities and Exchange Commission for a Dogecoin ETF. The filing is a significant step forward for cryptocurrency, which began life as a prank. If accepted, the ETF would allow ordinary investors to own exposure to Dogecoin without buying or owning the cryptocurrency itself.

According to regulatory filings, the new fund would be commodity-based, providing an alternative method of bringing Dogecoin into portfolios using traditional investment vehicles.21Shares Announces Partnership With Dogecoin Foundation

21Shares also announced that it has partnered with the House of Doge, the official business entity of the Dogecoin Foundation. From reports, the partnership is said to further entrench Dogecoin with conventional financial systems.

The alliance brings a new legitimacy to the currency, with traditional financial institutions now viewing it as a legitimate asset class and not merely an internet fad. Institutional support may entice more risk-averse investors who shunned the meme-coin in the past.New Exchange-Traded Product Launches With Physical Backing

In a further demonstration of its dedication to Dogecoin, 21Shares has introduced an exchange-traded product fully supported by the Dogecoin Foundation. This investment product will be collateralized by real Dogecoin in a 1:1 ratio, such that every share equates to holding real cryptocurrency in cold storage.

The firm will charge a management fee of 0.25% for this product, which is fairly competitive against peer cryptocurrency investment products. This physical backing model provides investors with confidence that their investment holds a real-world basis in the form of coins and not synthetic derivatives.

Featured image from Unsplash, chart from TradingView

data.title: Morgan Stanley cuts PTs on Australian travel cos on geopolitical uncertainty
data.description:
** Morgan Stanley trims PT for three Australian travel intermediaries, citing headwinds for industry amid increased geopolitical uncertainty

** Cuts PTs on Flight Centre Travel Group to A$16.60 from A$21.50, on Corporate Travel Management to A$15.30 from A$18.30, on Webjet to A$3.40 from A$3.70

** Says corporate customers concerns over heightened uncertainty, impact on leisure demand are valid

** FLT, CTD, WEB fall between 3.5% and 5.6% amid broader global market downturn as sentiment sours on trade war

** S&P/ASX 200 sheds 1.5%
